prompt:
You are the Architect agent drafting a typed Method artifact for an architecture project, following Juval Lowy's The Method to the letter.
Respond with a single JSON object. No prose outside the JSON.
Target artifact: Glossary
Your JSON output MUST conform exactly to this JSON Schema. Emit every required field; for an optional object field, emit the object or null — never a bare string in its place.
Schema: {"properties":{"items":{"items":{"properties":{"term":{"type":"string"},"definition":{"type":"string"},"category":{"type":"string"}},"additionalProperties":false,"type":"object","required":["term","definition","category"]},"type":"array"}},"additionalProperties":false,"type":"object","required":["items"]}

Mission context:
{"vision":"A connected community where local events organize themselves around the people who care.","objectives":[{"number":1,"statement":"Sustain organizer retention above 70% quarter-over-quarter by absorbing changes in notification channels, identity providers, and venue data sources without disrupting the organizing experience."},{"number":2,"statement":"Achieve and maintain member RSVP-to-attendance conversion above 60% across all active events, demonstrating that engagement mechanisms reliably translate intent into participation."},{"number":3,"statement":"Onboard new notification channels, identity providers, or venue data integrations within one release cycle and at no more than 15% incremental engineering cost, proving the platform's adaptability to a shifting external ecosystem."}],"mission":"The system encapsulates the volatility of how communities connect, participate, and gather behind a stable core of interacting components. An organizing engine governs the lifecycle of events and their relationships to members, delegating to a pluggable notification dispatcher whose channel implementations evolve independently of the engagement logic it serves. An identity broker abstracts the chosen identity provider so that member and organizer relationships remain constant as the underlying provider changes. A venue resolver mediates between events and external venue data sources, allowing those sources to be swapped without altering the event's structure. These components collaborate so that engagement (RSVP, comment, reminder) is orchestrated centrally while the channels, identities, and venues through which it flows remain interchangeable—enabling the platform to evolve its external relationships continuously from initial draft through deployment."}

Task: Extract the system's ubiquitous-language terms, each categorised by the Four Questions: Who interacts with the system, What is required of it, How (the business activity), Where (state lives). Define each term crisply in business language with NO solution/implementation wording. These terms are the shared vocabulary every later artifact must reuse verbatim.

items.definition: A community member who creates and governs the lifecycle of local events, inviting and engaging others to gather.
A community participant who discovers events, expresses intent to attend, and participates in the gathering.
An external party that vouches for who a member or organizer is, serving as the source of recognized identity.
An external party that supplies information about places where events may be held.
An external means through which the community is reached when events and engagement occur.
The requirement to manage an event from initial draft through its active life to conclusion, including its relationships to members.
The requirement that members' intent to attend reliably translates into participation.
The requirement to absorb changes in notification channels, identity providers, and venue data sources without disrupting the organizing experience.
The requirement that member and organizer relationships remain constant even as the underlying identity provider changes.
The requirement that the place of an event can be sourced or changed without altering the event's structure.
The business activity of governing events and their relationships to members over time.
The business activity of coordinating RSVP, comment, and reminder so that intent to attend is captured and nurtured into participation.
The business activity of reaching the community about events and engagement, independent of which channel is used.
The business activity of mediating recognition of members and organizers regardless of the chosen identity provider.
The business activity of mediating between an event and external information about where it is held.
A member's expressed intent to attend a given event.
Where the enduring facts about an event and its relationships to members reside.
Where the state of members' RSVPs, comments, and reminders for events is held.
Where the enduring association between members or organizers and their recognized identity is kept.
Where the association between an event and its resolved place is retained.
